title: Mildly Spicy Greek Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Greek lamb stew with a silky texture and a mild spicy kick, perfect for a hangover cure. This dish is jain-friendly and features fermented ingredients like nuka-pickle and hot sauce.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the vegetable broth, nuka-pickle, hot sauce, oregano, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
